name: delegate_to_agent
description: Start an independent local sub-agent for a self-contained task. ASYNCHRONOUS: returns task_id immediately. The child starts cold and cannot see this conversation, open files, or earlier turns, so task must include all context. Fan out independent work, then Join every still-required returned task_id with get_delegation_status (return_when=all_terminal_or_attention, wait_ms=0) before ending the turn. For each distinct delegation profile mentioned as codeg://delegation-profile/<uuid>, call once with its UUID as profile_id.

agent_type.description: Local agent to run the task.

profile_id.description: Optional immutable profile UUID. Runs exactly that profile without falling back to agent defaults.

profile_label.description: Display label for the explicit profile mention; does not affect routing.

task.description: Complete prompt: goal, context, absolute paths, constraints, and expected output.

working_dir.description: Absolute working directory; defaults to the parent session directory.

name: get_delegation_status
description: Get status or results for task_ids. Omit wait_ms for an immediate snapshot. To block, Join with return_when=all_terminal_or_attention and explicit wait_ms=0; positive wait_ms is rejected on coordination_v1. Join returns when all requested tasks are terminal, requested-child attention is required, or the Broker is unavailable. Answer every attention request, then re-Join only still-running required task ids. Returns {"tasks":[...]} in input order; Join responses also include wake_reason and attention_requests.

task_ids.description: One or more task IDs. One checks a single task; several check a fan-out together.

wait_ms.description: Omit wait_ms for an immediate snapshot. Use 0 with return_when=all_terminal_or_attention for canonical Join. Positive wait_ms is rejected on coordination_v1.

return_when.description: Canonical Join condition. Requires explicit wait_ms=0; returns for all terminal, attention required, or unavailable.

name: cancel_delegation
description: Cancel a running task only when its result is no longer wanted. timeout is non-canceling and returns guidance to keep waiting. Prefer get_delegation_status with wait_ms for slow work. If the task already finished, return its final result.

reason.description: Reason: timeout is non-canceling; taskfail, usercancel, and others cancel.

name: check_user_feedback
description: Read pending user guidance during this turn; messages are available only through this tool. Non-blocking and immediate. Call before starting implementation, before a significant decision, and after a meaningful sub-task. Treat returned messages as HIGH-PRIORITY steering; an empty result means continue.

name: ask_user_question
description: Render interactive cards for 1-4 related multiple-choice questions and BLOCK until submitted or dismissed. Use only for a genuinely user-owned discrete decision that cannot be resolved from the request, code, or sensible defaults. Do not ask merely whether to proceed, confirm an obvious default, or collect open-ended input. Other is added automatically. Put a recommended choice first and append ' (Recommended)' to its label. Prefer one call for related questions.

multiSelect.description: Whether multiple options may be selected.

label.description: Short choice text; append ' (Recommended)' to the first recommended choice.

description.description: Meaning or trade-off of this choice.

name: get_session_info
description: Resolve a codeg://session/<number> reference to read-only metadata and optional recent messages. Call when a mentioned session is needed to continue or summarize its work. Pass Codeg's internal conversation ID as session_id, not the agent session ID. A missing session returns found: false, not an error.

session_id.description: Number from codeg://session/<number>; Codeg's internal conversation ID.

max_messages.description: Recent turns to include. Defaults to 20; 0 returns metadata only; max 200.

name: request_parent_decision
description: Ask the direct parent for a blocking decision. Blocks until reply or closure; not for progress, logs, or warnings.

name: reply_to_delegation
description: Reply to an open direct-child Join decision. First reply wins; same reply is idempotent.
